mechanism_narrative: After a series of rate hikes, a pause or reversal in the Federal Funds Rate signals a monetary policy pivot. This reduces the discount rate for future corporate earnings and eases financial conditions, leading to a positive re-rating of equities over the medium term.
evidence_refs: fact:The Federal Reserve hiked rates throughout 2018, with the last hike in December 2018.
failure_condition: In the 6 months following a pause after a hiking cycle, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: A persistently low unemployment rate below its natural rate indicates a tight labor market, which supports robust consumer spending and corporate earnings. This positive economic momentum carries forward into near-term equity returns.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 3.9% is near a 50-year low, indicating a very tight labor market.
failure_condition: The information coefficient of the UNRATE level for predicting 20-day forward returns is negative over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in both retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) provides a powerful joint signal of a broad-based contraction in consumer demand and confidence. This precedes negative revisions to corporate earnings expectations, leading to negative equity returns.
candidate_trigger: 3-month change in RSAFS < 0 AND 3-month change in HOUST < 0
evidence_refs: fact:Housing market showed signs of slowing in late 2018 due to higher mortgage rates.
failure_condition: When both RSAFS and HOUST are declining over a 3-month period, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of occurrences.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month decline in industrial production (INDPRO) signals a contraction in the manufacturing and supply side of the economy. This shock negatively impacts corporate revenues and profit margins, leading to lower equity prices, especially when growth is already slowing.
evidence_refs: fact:Global manufacturing PMIs and trade tensions were a concern in late 2018.
failure_condition: The hit rate of the signal (correctly predicting negative returns) is less than 55% over a 5-year walk-forward period.

mechanism_narrative: Strong momentum in nonfarm payrolls growth reflects robust economic activity and corporate hiring. This translates directly into higher expected aggregate demand and corporate profits, boosting equity returns over the very short term.
evidence_refs: fact:The most recent PAYEMS report for Dec-2018 showed a very strong gain of 312k jobs.
failure_condition: The rank correlation between the 3-month change in PAYEMS and 5-day forward S&P 500 returns is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) over a 10-year period.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation (CPIAUCSL) accelerates while the policy rate (FEDFUNDS) rises more slowly, the real interest rate falls. This signals the central bank is 'behind the curve', risking an overheating economy and a more aggressive future tightening cycle, which negatively impacts equity markets.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL YoY % change > 2.5% AND FEDFUNDS < CPIAUCSL YoY % change
failure_condition: In periods where inflation is above target and the real Fed Funds rate is negative, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive more than 60% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating year-over-year real GDP growth indicates a strengthening economic expansion. This fundamental improvement in the macroeconomic backdrop leads to upward revisions in long-term earnings forecasts, supporting higher equity valuations over the next quarter.
evidence_refs: fact:Real GDP growth was solid through Q3 2018, providing a strong economic base.
failure_condition: The correlation between the year-over-year change in GDPC1 and 60-day forward S&P 500 returns is negative over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle economy with a very low unemployment rate (UNRATE), an upside surprise in inflation (CPIAUCSL) forces the market to price in a more hawkish central bank response. This expectation of higher future interest rates leads to an immediate de-rating of equity multiples.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE is 3.9% as of the decision point, meeting the tight labor market condition.
failure_condition: When the trigger conditions are met, the S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of instances.
